repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Range Rover P400 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Replace all six — simple, effective preventative maintenance
Worth it on a well-maintained Range Rover
Replacement often £5,500–£8,500 — assess vehicle value carefully

buyingChecks.title: Buying This Range Rover P400? Check These First
buyIf: * Full Land Rover service history with documented oil changes (12,000 miles max)
* Post-2021 L460 model — the newer chassis and electronics are more refined
* Documented ignition coil replacement (six coils replaced at once)
* Recent 48V MHEV system software updates applied
avoidIf: * Any evidence of misfire or hesitation without a documented fix (coil risk)
* Poor service history — the Ingenium I6 is less forgiving of neglect than the old V8s
* 48V warnings showing without a diagnosed repair
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis — these engines can hide expensive problems

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The P400's 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six is one of JLR's best modern engines — smooth, powerful and efficient. It's a significant step forward from the earlier Ford-sourced V6 and V8s. The coil-on-plug ignition system is the main wear item, but it's cheap and easy to fix. Buy on history, maintain the 48V system, and the P400 will serve you well.

cta.label: → Compare Range Rover P400 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Range Rover P400 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for any rattle on startup (timing chain stretch warning)
* Service History — 12,000 mile oil changes are essential; 8,000–10,000 is safer
* Misfire Check — Any hesitation, rough idle, or engine warning light? (coil failure)
* 48V System — Any battery warnings or start-stop issues? (BISG module)
* Direct Injection Carbon — Has the intake had a walnut blast service? (every 60k miles recommended)
* Oil Consumption — Monitor between services; the Ingenium I6 is generally not thirsty
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Coil and MHEV codes are common
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (ignition/catalyst issues)

items.question: How much does a Land Rover Range Rover P400 engine replacement cost in the UK?
items.answer: A reconditioned Range Rover P400 engine (AJ20P6 / PT306) typically costs £5,500–£8,500 supply only. Fitting adds £1,400–£2,200. These are specialist-only replacements — the Ingenium 3.0L I6 requires Land Rover specialist expertise. An ignition coil replacement costs £350–£600.

items.question: Is the Land Rover Range Rover P400 reliable?
items.answer: The P400's 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six is JLR's most advanced petrol engine. It's significantly more refined than the earlier V6s and has proven generally reliable. The main wear items are ignition coils (every 40,000–80,000 miles) and the 48V mild-hybrid BISG module. Regular oil changes (12,000 miles max) are essential to prevent timing chain stretch.

items.question: What are the common problems with the Range Rover P400?
items.answer: Ignition coil failure (misfire, rough idle, £350–£600 repair), 48V mild-hybrid BISG module faults (battery warnings, £800–£2,500 repair), and timing chain stretch on higher-mileage or poorly maintained examples (£2,000–£4,500 repair). Direct injector coking also occurs — walnut blasting every 60,000 miles is recommended.

items.question: Is it worth replacing a Range Rover P400 engine, or should I scrap the car?
items.answer: Range Rover P400 values range from £45,000–£150,000 depending on year, condition and mileage. A £5,500–£8,500 reconditioned engine represents 5–20% of vehicle value — well within our 60% Land Rover scrap threshold. For most examples, replacement is financially viable.

items.question: How do I find my Land Rover Range Rover P400 engine code?
items.answer: The P400 uses the AJ20P6 (also known as PT306) engine code — JLR's 3.0-litre Ingenium inline-six with 48V mild-hybrid. This is shared across L405 (2019–2021) and L460 (2021–present) Range Rovers. The code is stamped on the engine block; your VIN and vehicle documentation will also confirm the specification.

items.question: Can I repair the ignition coil issue, or does it need replacement?
items.answer: Yes — coil replacement is straightforward. The most common cause of P400 misfires is failed ignition coils. Replace all six coils and spark plugs together (£350–£600) as they degrade at similar rates. Symptoms include rough idle, hesitation, and worse performance in damp conditions. At the first sign of misfire, book an inspection — ignored misfires can damage the catalytic converter.
